paper.title_en: Quantum stability criterion for <math><mi>α</mi></math>-clustering: A generalized harmonic oscillator approach with pairing and spin-orbit interactions
paper.abstract_en: We present a theoretical framework for understanding α-clustering in even-even self-conjugate (<math><mrow><mi>N</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>Z</mi></mrow></math>) nuclei through a deformed harmonic oscillator approach that incorporates schematic nuclear interactions. The work introduces three enhancements to conventional single-particle gap analyses: (1) a generalized four-nucleon stability criterion <math><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><msubsup><mi>E</mi><mrow><mtext>clus</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>(</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow></msubsup><mo>=</mo><mo movablelimits="true" form="prefix">min</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><mn>4</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><mi>E</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>−</mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mtext>pair</mtext></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><mi>E</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>−</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>E</mi><mtext>pair</mtext></msub><mo>)</mo></mrow></mrow></math> that accounts for the correlated nature of alpha clusters, (2) the incorporation of a schematic spin-orbit interaction <math><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>SO</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mo>−</mo><mi>κ</mi><mi>ℏ</mi><msub><mi>ω</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mrow><mo>[</mo><mi mathvariant="bold">l</mi><mo>·</mo><mi mathvariant="bold">s</mi><mo>]</mo></mrow></mrow></math> that breaks degeneracies, and (3) a BCS-inspired pairing correction <math><mrow><msub><mi>E</mi><mtext>pair</mtext></msub><mo>=</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><mo>−</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><mn>0</mn><mn>2</mn></msubsup><mo>/</mo><mi>G</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo form="prefix">exp</mo><mrow><mo>[</mo><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>G</mi><mi>ρ</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>F</mi></msub><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo>]</mo></mrow></mrow></math> that modifies cluster stability near the Fermi surface. Using this framework we compute cluster stability maps for a complete set of self-conjugate nuclei from <math><mrow><mmultiscripts><mi>He</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></mmultiscripts></mrow></math> to <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Ge</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>64</mn></mmultiscripts></math>, documenting the evolution of α-cluster correlations across the nuclear chart. The results indicate that while the overall magnitude of cluster stability decreases with increasing mass number due to growing level density, localized regions of enhanced stability persist at specific deformations that correspond to experimentally observed cluster configurations. The spin-orbit interaction selectively suppresses certain configurations while preserving the most robust ones, offering a possible explanation as to why some harmonic oscillator predictions are not observed experimentally. Pairing effects become increasingly important in heavier systems (<math><mrow><mi>A</mi><mo>></mo><mn>40</mn></mrow></math>), sometimes enhancing stability at large deformations through superfluid-type correlations. This systematic study provides a qualitative bridge between simple harmonic oscillator insights and realistic mean-field predictions, offering a useful exploratory framework for interpreting cluster structures in self-conjugate nuclei.
